name: Orjaleves (Orja Soup)
recipeInstructions:
This is a soup prepared on pig harvest day and is the kind that has to be made in large quantities.
Wash beef bones. Cook bones and flank in 3 quarts cold water with 1 tablespoon salt. After 30 minutes, add onion, garlic, paprika, peppercorns, and cloves. Continue boiling for an additional hour.
Put 3 quarts water in a separate pot with pork head section. Boil for 4 minutes. Remove pork head, rinse, and set aside until beef is finished. Then add pork head and backbone to the beef. Cook until done.
Meanwhile, peel vegetables and cut into long strips. Using 2 cups of the meat broth, cook vegetables. Add saffron, celery, and parsley leaves.
Make a dough with flour and egg; roll it as thin as possible. Cut into 3-inch strips and roll around rolling pin in spiral. Slide spirals onto baking sheet and dry in an oven set to 200°F for about 10 minutes.
Take soup from heat, remove meat and bones. Remove pork meat from bones, cut into small pieces. Reserve the boiled beef flank as a main course or save for another occasion. Skim soup to remove fat. Remove vegetables; set aside. Strain broth through cheesecloth. Cook spiral noodles in soup just prior to serving.
Put pork meat in soup tureen, add cooked vegetables, and pour in broth with noodles on top.
